content:
Formatting
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

Goal
* Please format all [[variables]].

* Please format all {{constants}}, including 9.80665, "HIT" and "MISS".

* Please change "<error>" to "[[error]]".

* Use <<bold>> to highlight certain text if appropriate.

* [[R]] is undefined.

Input
* Please format "Line 1" in <<bold>>.

* Please add "Three space-separated values" before "V0 theta D".

* Please format all [[variables]].

* Please delete "0 < theta < 90" (a similar constraint has been stated in the Constraints section).

* Please change the first two "float" to "integer", as V0 and theta are integers in all existing cases. (Alternatively, include cases where V0 and theta are floats.)

Output
* Please format "HIT" and "MISS" as {{constants}}.

* Please change "<error>" to "[[error]]".

* Please format all [[variables]].

* [[R]] is undefined.

Constraints
* Please format all [[variables]].

* Please change 200 to 100 as V0 never exceeds 100.

* Please change 5000 to 1000 as D never exceeds 1000.

Stub generator
* Please change the first two "float" to "int".

* Please add a write statement, e.g.
write HIT/MISS

Cases
* Each test and its validator should cover the same scenario with slightly different inputs (and different outputs if relevant and possible). Please revise the existing eight cases to become eight pairs of cases (one pair = one test + one validator).

Please let me know when you've finished, and I'll take a more in-depth review.

Thanks.

Hi, thanks for telling me everything !
This is my first post and I just edited it, it is good now ?

No worries. This is already looking much better. Thanks for the improvements so far. There are a few more adjustments needed:

Goal
* Consider adding "(inclusive)" after "0.5m" for clarity.

Output
* Consider adding "(inclusive)" after "0.5m" for clarity.

Cases
* Please name all the validators. For example, you could use "Validator 1/2/3/etc.", or reuse the same name as the corresponding test.

* Thank you for restructuring the cases into pairs that share the same V0 and theta. That's definitely a step in the right direction.

However, there's still an important issue with the current coverage. I apologise if my earlier comment ("different outputs") caused any misunderstanding.

At the moment:
• Every test (visible in the IDE) expects HIT
• Every validator (hidden) expects MISS

Tests are meant to help players verify their solutions before submission, while validators are used to assess the submitted code. Ideally, each pair should represent the same logical outcome (either both HIT or both MISS), with slightly different inputs to improve robustness.

Would you please revise the cases again based on this clarification?

To summarise, for each pair:
• Each input may be the same or slightly different
• The output should both be HIT or both be MISS.
• If both are MISS, the corresponding [[error]] values should differ slightly.

Thank you again.

Thanks again !
Normally now its good ^^
Looks good to me. I'll let you know again if I think of anything else 🙂
